title: Robust chemical analysis with graphene chemosensors and machine learning
data_sources: In-house graphene ISFET electrical measurements (Penn State University)

data_scope.primary_data: graphene ISFET transfer characteristics across multiple chips and analytes
data_scope.analytes_tested: pH buffers, adulterated milk, wine/water varieties, fish freshness time series, PFAS (PFHxA at 2.5/25/250/2500 ppb)
data_scope.chip_count: multiple (exact count not stated in abstract/figures)

core_conclusion: Non-functionalized graphene-based ISFET arrays paired with ANNs achieve robust classification and quantification of complex chemical solutions, detecting food fraud, food spoilage, and PFAS contamination at sub-ppb levels. Machine learning mitigates cycle-to-cycle, sensor-to-sensor, and chip-to-chip variability while SHAP analysis provides interpretable insight into which electrical features drive each discrimination task, enabling commercial-scale chemosensing without functionalization or recalibration.
why_it_matters: Turns hardware variability from a barrier into a solved problem; demonstrates chemosensing without functionalization via data-driven selectivity; addresses urgent food-safety (milk adulteration, wine fraud, fish spoilage) and environmental threats (PFAS); uses SHAP as a scientific instrument to reveal new ISFET physics; fully open data and code on GitHub.
why_nature: Broad cross-disciplinary reach spanning materials science, electrical engineering, food science, and ML; platform-level contribution generalizable across analytes and industries; clear societal relevance in food safety and environmental monitoring; hardware-software co-design novelty rather than incremental engineering.
added_value_to_my_research: Federated ISFET chip-to-chip generalization -> federated spatial ML across cities/regions with restricted data sharing
SHAP attribution stability as sensor-health diagnostic -> SHAP drift as data-quality flag in spatiotemporal geospatial models
Distributed ISFET node network -> spatially interpolated contamination maps using MGWR/KDE for public health exposure assessment
Physics-informed ISFET learning (Nernst-constrained ANN) -> physics-informed graph learning with spatial conservation laws
Multimodal ISFET + gas spectroscopy fusion -> multi-source geospatial data fusion (mobile phone + remote sensing + social media)
